template.name: Template App PgBouncer
template.description: Check PgBouncer status and LLD pools. Check pools metrics. https://www.pgbouncer.org/usage.html#command-line-switches https://github.com/Lelik13a/Zabbix-PgBouncer

item.name: Total free client connections
item.description: Count of free clients. These are clients that are disconnected, but PgBouncer keeps the memory around that was allocated for them so it can be reused for a future clients to avoid allocations.

item.name: Total free server connections
item.description: Count of free servers. These are servers that are disconnected, but PgBouncer keeps the memory around that was allocated for them so it can be reused for a future servers to avoid allocations.

item.name: Total login client connections
item.description: Count of clients in login state.

item.name: Average number of SQL queries among all databases
Average query duration duration among all databases
Time spent by clients waiting for a server among all databases
item.description: Time spent by clients waiting for a server, in seconds (average of the wait times for clients assigned a backend during the current stats_period).

trigger.name: Time spent by clients waiting for a server more then {$PGBOUNCER.WAIT.TIME}
trigger.description: Time spent by clients waiting for a server, in seconds (average of the wait times for clients assigned a backend during the current stats_period) more then {$PGBOUNCER.WAIT.TIME}

item.name: Average number of SQL transactions among all databases
Average transaction duration among all databases
Total received data from clients among all databases
item.description: Total received data from clients among all databases

item.name: Number of SQL queries among all databases per sec
Total sent data to clients among all databases
item.description: Total sent data to clients among all databases

item.name: Number of SQL transactions among all databases per sec

item_prototype.name: Current client connections for DB {#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}
item_prototype.description: Current number of client connections for this database.

item_prototype.name: Current connections for DB {#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}
item_prototype.description: Current number of server connections for this database.

item_prototype.name: Maximum connections for DB {#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}
item_prototype.description: Maximum number of allowed server connections for this database, as set by max_db_connections, either globally or per database.

item_prototype.name: Pool size for DB {#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}
item_prototype.description: Maximum number of server connections.

item_prototype.name: PgBouncer {#PGBOUNCER_USER}@{#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}: client maxwait
item_prototype.description: How long the first (oldest) client in the queue has waited, in seconds. If this starts increasing, then the current pool of servers does not handle requests quickly enough. The reason may be either an overloaded server or just too small of a pool_size setting.

trigger_prototype.name: PgBouncer {#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE} client max wait time over {$PGBOUNCER.POOLS.MAXWAIT.TIME}
trigger_prototype.description: How long the first (oldest) client in the queue has waited, in seconds. If this starts increasing, then the current pool of servers does not handle requests quickly enough. The reason may be either an overloaded server or just too small of a pool_size setting.

item_prototype.name: PgBouncer client {#PGBOUNCER_USER}@{#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}: active connections
item_prototype.description: Client connections that are either linked to server connections or are idle with no queries waiting to be processed.

item_prototype.name: PgBouncer client {#PGBOUNCER_USER}@{#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}: active waiting
item_prototype.description: Client connections that have sent queries but have not yet got a server connection.

item_prototype.name: PgBouncer client {#PGBOUNCER_USER}@{#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}: maxwait
item_prototype.description: How long the first (oldest) client in the queue has waited, in seconds. If this starts increasing, then the current pool of servers does not handle requests quickly enough. The reason may be either an overloaded server or just too small of a pool_size setting.

item_prototype.name: PgBouncer client {#PGBOUNCER_USER}@{#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}: maxwait micro seconds
item_prototype.description: How long the first (oldest) client in the queue has waited, in seconds. If this starts increasing, then the current pool of servers does not handle requests quickly enough. The reason may be either an overloaded server or just too small of a pool_size setting. Microsecond part of the maximum waiting time.

item_prototype.name: PgBouncer client {#PGBOUNCER_USER}@{#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}: pool mode
item_prototype.description: The pooling mode in use

item_prototype.name: PgBouncer server {#PGBOUNCER_USER}@{#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}: active connections
item_prototype.description: Server connections that are linked to a client.

item_prototype.name: PgBouncer server {#PGBOUNCER_USER}@{#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}: unused connections
item_prototype.description: Server connections that are unused and immediately usable for client queries.

item_prototype.name: PgBouncer server {#PGBOUNCER_USER}@{#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}: logging connections
item_prototype.description: Server connections currently in the process of logging in.

item_prototype.name: PgBouncer server {#PGBOUNCER_USER}@{#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}: tested connections
item_prototype.description: Server connections that are currently running either server_reset_query or server_check_query.

item_prototype.name: PgBouncer server {#PGBOUNCER_USER}@{#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}: used connections
item_prototype.description: Server connections that have been idle for more than server_check_delay, so they need server_check_query to run on them before they can be used again.

item_prototype.name: Average query duration for DB {#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}
item_prototype.description: Average query duration, in seconds.

item_prototype.name: Average wait time for DB {#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}
item_prototype.description: Time spent by clients waiting for a server, in seconds (average of the wait times for clients assigned a backend during the current stats_period).

trigger_prototype.name: PgBouncer {#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE} wait time over {$PGBOUNCER.WAIT.TIME}
trigger_prototype.description: Time spent by clients waiting for a server, in seconds (average per second).

item_prototype.name: Average transaction duration for DB {#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}
item_prototype.description: Average transaction duration, in seconds.

item_prototype.name: Number of SQL queries/s for DB {#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}
item_prototype.description: Total number of SQL commands pooled by pgbouncer.

item_prototype.name: Received data for DB {#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}
item_prototype.description: Network traffic received by pgbouncer in bytes.

item_prototype.name: Sent data for DB {#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}
item_prototype.description: Network traffic sent by pgbouncer in bytes.

item_prototype.name: Number of SQL transactions/s for DB {#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE}
item_prototype.description: Total number of SQL transactions pooled by pgbouncer.

trigger_prototype.name: Client connections for DB {#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE} over {$PGBOUNCER.CL.CONN.WARN}% used
trigger_prototype.description: pool_size Maximum number of server connections. current_client_connections Current number of client connections for this database.

trigger_prototype.name: Connections to DB {#PGBOUNCER_DATABASE} over {$PGBOUNCER.DB.CONN.WARN}% used
trigger_prototype.description: max_connections Maximum number of allowed server connections for this database, as set by max_db_connections, either globally or per database. current_connections Current number of server connections for this database.

macro.description: Percent for used client connections per database
Percent for used connections per database
How long the first (oldest) client in the queue has waited, in seconds.
Time spent by clients waiting for a server, in seconds onds.

value.name: PgBouncer: Total data flow among all databases

graph.name: PgBouncer: Total data flow among all databases
